sglang.title: Tune memory arguments on B200
sglang.author: Fridge003
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
B200 machines are equipped with much higher GBM than H200 (144G -> 184G)
Some of the server arguments can be tuned on B200 to improve the performance on large workload:

mem_fraction_static: set to 0.79
chunked_prefill_size: set to 16384
cuda_graph_bs: raise the upper limit to 512 (might

sglang.title: Add DeepSeek-R1-0528 function call chat template
sglang.author: Xu-Wenqing
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
DeepSeek-R1-0528 model support function call, add function call chat template.
Modifications
add DeepSeek-R1-0528 chat template for function call.
Usage:
python -m sglang.launch_server ... --tool-call-parser deepseekv3 --chat-template ./examples/chat_template/tool_chat_template_deepseekr1

Non-stream function call response in OpenAI doesn't have index. We should remove that. See: https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/object

vllm.title: [ROCm][V0][Attention] Revert to the previous FA triton kernel
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Revert to the previous version of the triton attention kernel, modified to support FP8 computation.
The kernel brought in in #12591 turned out to have performance issues, and broken support for FP8 quantized models.
Until that is resolved we want to replace it from the performant version from the RO

vllm.title: [BugFix] Make DP work with connector-delayed new requests
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The current DP engine logic assumes that the step() function will always perform a forward pass when there are any running and/or waiting requests. With the new async connector changes this might no longer be the case, specifically when there's only requests in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KV state.
These cha

vllm.author: wseaton
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Stacked PR, needs to go in after: #18559 Edit: Not really stacked, these changes can go in on their own, PD+DP just won't work without the scheduling bugfixes.
Changes:

Engine IDs for KVConnector are now generated in the factory if they are unset, in the DP case this means each thread gets it's own

vllm.title: Use standalone_compile by default in torch >= 2.8.0

This includes the current PyTorch nightlies.
Test Plan:

in #17057, I verified that running https://gist.github.com/zou3519/aebb622714e80f4cd4c369472f2372cd with or without VLLM_TEST_STANDALONE_COMPILE resulted in Inductor producing the same exact output code (via tlparse). I did this for the cold-s

vllm.title: [TPU] remove transpose ops in moe kernel
vllm.author: yaochengji
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The new MoE kernel now supports the rhs_transpose argument, which allows us to remove the external transpose operations that were previously a significant drain on execution time.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix PP default fallback behavior for V1
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We were not checking if the distributed_executor_backend was None (the default) to make the V0 fallback decision.
Before this PR you would see:
vllm serve meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --enforce-eager -pp 2   
...
WARNING 05-29 16:18:03 [arg_utils.py:1586] Pipeline Parallelism without Ray distrib

vllm.title: [Misc] Update type annotation for rotary embedding `base`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Previously, base was annotated as int type which caused a bunch of type checking errors when calling get_rope because rope_theta is annotated as float in each model. There's nothing preventing base from being a float inside the rotary embedding implementations.

vllm.title: improve the robustness of parsing vlms config in AutoRound
vllm.author: wenhuach21
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The latest Transformers update changed the module names of most VLMs, which led me to mistakenly think the name mismatch was caused by vLLM. To handle this, I used the in operator to match module names but that's not very safe.
